channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: House GOP leaders call on Rep. Gonzales to end reelection campaign
item.description: Tony Gonzales, a Republican from Texas, has admitted to an affair with an aide who later died after setting herself on fire.

item.title: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem is out, Trump says, after controversial tenure
item.description: President Donald Trump announced the decision on social media and said Noem would be replaced with Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.).

item.title: D.C.’s cherry blossoms will peak between March 29 and April 1, Park Service says
item.description: The National Park Service declares peak bloom when 70 percent of the blossoms around the Tidal Basin, the reservoir on the National Mall, have opened.

item.title: White House ballroom vote, a final procedural hurdle, has been delayed due to significant public input, federal commission says
item.description: The National Capital Planning Commission’s chair cited the amount of public comment as the reason for the delay.

item.title: Now streaming! The Clinton depositions were the week’s best new drama, our columnist writes.
item.description: Your favorite characters are back, and you won’t believe what the writers have in store for them this season.

item.title: Thousands of public comments slam Trump’s ballroom ahead of today’s hearing
item.description: A Post analysis of submitted comments found more than 97 percent were critical of the planned 90,000-square-foot addition. The White House has defended it as necessary.

item.title: The best argument for setting America free? A polymath nailed it.
item.description: If the crisis in America could have been resolved by words alone, Britain might have won an easy victory.

item.title: Trump offers U.S. air cover and other support to groups within Iran and Iraq if they rise up against Tehran
item.description: In calls this week to Kurdish minority leaders in Iran and neighboring Iraq, President Donald Trump offered “extensive U.S. aircover” and other backing for anti-regime Iranian Kurds to take over portions of western Iran, according to multiple people familiar with the effort.

item.title: A really big burger in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: Iran vote. Detention center. Ballroom complaints. Pam Bondi. RFK Jr. Supplement advice. Flight annoyance. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: RFK Jr.’s dunk on Dunkin’ riles up New Englanders: ‘Come and take it’
item.description: “We’re going to ask Dunkin’ Donuts and Starbucks, ‘Show us the safety data that show that it’s okay for a teenage girl to drink an iced coffee with 115 grams of sugar in it,’” the health secretary said recently.

item.title: ICE taking steps to close massive immigrant detention center at Fort Bliss, document shows
item.description: Preparations to shutter Camp East Montana in El Paso come less than eight months after facility’s opening, according to an internal ICE document -- ending a tumultuous experiment in large-scale detention where three detainees died.

item.title: Senate rejects resolution to force Trump to end Iran strikes
item.description: Democrats — along with Sen. Rand Paul (R-Kentucky) — forced a vote on the war powers resolution over the opposition of most Republicans, who control the Senate.

item.title: Lou Holtz dies at 89. The Hall of Fame college football coach led Notre Dame to a national title and also served as a TV analyst.
item.description: As the head coach at six colleges, he had a knack for transforming struggling teams into winners. He guided Notre Dame to a perfect 12-0 record and the 1988 national championship.

item.title: House Oversight Committee votes to subpoena Attorney General Pam Bondi over handling of Epstein investigation
item.description: Bondi has faced criticism from Democrats, and some Republicans, over the Justice Department’s handling of the investigation of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and his associates in compliance with the Epstein Files Transparency Act.

item.title: Putin is failing. These charts prove it.
item.description: Putin is failing. These charts prove it.

item.title: As war shakes stock markets, here’s what you should (and shouldn’t) do with your money, our columnist writes
item.description: For most investors, regardless of age, the key to weathering a jumpy market is to avoid fear-driven trades, Michelle Singletary advises.

item.title: It’s been so cold that cherry blossoms’ peak bloom may push to latest date in years
item.description: Capital Weather Gang is predicting a peak bloom date for the Tidal Basin’s Yoshino trees between April 3 and 7, which would be the latest since 2018 (April 5). If the trees peak even later, that could surpass 2015 (April 10).

item.title: Is platonic female friend killing my chances at love? Carolyn Hax readers give advice.
item.description: Is platonic female friend killing my chances at love? Carolyn Hax readers give advice.

item.title: Rep. Tony Gonzales faces ethics investigation over allegations of affair with aide
item.description: The House Ethics Committee will investigate allegations that Gonzales (R-Texas) had an affair with a former staff member who later died after setting herself on fire, ensuring that the scandal that has dogged Gonzales through a bitter primary race will continue to factor heavily as he heads into a runoff.

item.title: Nasty GOP race in Texas heads to a runoff, older Democrats face new-guard challengers, and Trump’s influence ousts an incumbent
item.description: A heated and intensely personal Republican primary for a U.S. Senate seat in Texas won’t be over until May 26: Sen. John Cornyn will head to a runoff against the state’s firebrand attorney general, Ken Paxton. In North Carolina’s Senate race, Michael Whatley, a former Republican National Committee chairman, will face off against the state’s former Democratic governor, Roy Cooper.

item.title: The Democratic establishment gets the night it wanted
item.description: Good nights in Texas and North Carolina offer relief — but the progressive challenge is far from over.

item.title: A closed-door deposition in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Wednesday points.

item.title: U.S. submarine torpedo sank Iranian warship in Indian Ocean, Hegseth says. Sri Lanka says about 180 people were aboard.
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the incident occurred Tuesday. Citing the Sri Lankan navy, he said more than 30 people had been rescued.

item.title: NATO shoots down Iranian missile heading toward Turkey’s airspace, condemns Iran
item.description: It was unclear whether the missile was targeting Turkey, a NATO member state bordering Iran, but if it was, it would mark a major escalation in the war.

item.title: Iran conflict. Stock market. Primary results. School shooting case. Flu vs. covid. Warm weather. Overthinking. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Pentagon releases names of four U.S. service members killed in Iran war
item.description: The Department of Defense identified four of the six U.S. military members who have been killed since the war began. The four died Sunday in Kuwait in a drone attack, the Pentagon said.

item.title: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick to appear before House panel investigating Epstein
item.description: Lutnick, who was once Jeffrey Epstein’s neighbor, will voluntarily sit for an interview before the House Oversight Committee, its chairman said.

item.title: McDonald’s new Big Arch burger is an ode to American excess
item.description: The newcomer goes big with two quarter-pound patties, three slices of cheese and more than 1,000 calories.

item.title: Inside the dirty, difficult and putrid repair job after the Potomac sewage spill
item.description: The Post was given exclusive access to watch the crews work on pipes clogged with non-disposable wipes and a wall of rocks, among other things, in the freezing cold.

item.title: An economic transformation is coming to America’s heartland
item.description: Cities like Columbus and Denver offer something uniquely American that the coasts do not.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Tell wife about that growing neck hump from her bad laptop habits?
item.description: Letter writer thinks wife’s unattractive neck hump is fixable, but how does he tell her that delicately?

item.title: Father of accused Georgia school shooter is found guilty of murder in landmark case against a parent
item.description: Colin Gray, 55, faces a maximum of 180 years in prison after a Barrow County jury found him guilty of 29 counts, including two counts of second-degree murder and two counts of involuntary manslaughter.

item.title: The Trump Doctrine is here, Marc A. Thiessen writes
item.description: Like Reagan, the president has found a way to change the world without putting boots on the ground.

item.title: How threats against Trump’s life played into his decision to attack Iran and its ayatollah
item.description: President Donald Trump for the first time acknowledged a personal dimension to his decision to attack Iran, citing in an ABC News interview the country’s efforts to assassinate him as a factor in ordering the joint U.S.-Israeli operation that killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: A primary race in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Tuesday points.

item.title: U.S. closes two gulf embassies as Iran widens strikes. Follow our live coverage.
item.description: The embassies in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait were shut after drone attacks as Iran’s Revolutionary Guard launched strikes around the region Tuesday. Israel carried out fresh attacks against high-profile targets in Iran and said it would deploy ground troops deeper into Lebanon, as Hezbollah said its militants targeted Israel with drones.

item.title: Iran conflict. Trump poll. Clinton depositions. Midterms. Supreme Court. ICE training. Test your strength. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: National Park Service database flags hundreds of items that might ‘disparage’ America
item.description: An internal government database reviewed by The Washington Post demonstrates the vast scope of the Trump administration’s ongoing effort to revise or remove information on African American history, climate change and other topics at hundreds of national park sites.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Longtime PTA partner rants about the ‘horrors of working with me’
item.description: The two put the PTA on their backs for 15 years. The letter writer just learned how the other really feels about working together.

item.title: We texted 1,000 Americans about U.S. strikes in Iran. Here’s what they said.
item.description: More Americans oppose the strikes than support them, a flash poll found. Perceptions of Trump’s goals vary widely, though a clear majority say his administration has not clearly explained them. Still, about half think the U.S. military’s actions will contribute to long-term U.S. security.

item.title: This person’s sartorial choices are the topic of today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Monday points.

item.title: The economy is changing. Don’t forget who fears it most.
item.description: Artificial intelligence doomer narratives reflect personal concerns, not economic reality.

item.title: U.S. expects more casualties, Joint Chiefs Chairman Dan Caine says, as Iran war widens. A 4th U.S. service member has died.
item.description: The U.S. military expects to take additional casualties in its operation against Iran, Caine said in a rare on-camera news briefing, adding that accomplishing objectives will take “some time" and “in some cases, will be difficult and gritty work.”

item.title: Iran attack. Democrats’ response. Texas primary. AI newspaper. Winter blast. Microplastics. Lunar eclipse. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: 3 U.S. jets downed in ‘apparent friendly fire’; Middle East conflict spreads to Lebanon. Follow our live coverage.
item.description: The fighter jets went down Sunday night over Kuwait, U.S. Central Command said, amid active combat in which all six crew members safely ejected. The Middle East conflict escalated as Iran’s salvos against Persian Gulf states continued and Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Israel, prompting Israeli strikes on the group's strongholds in Lebanon.

item.title: Trump leaves room to claim victory — or avoid blame — in recorded Iran strike messages
item.description: President Donald Trump has taken an approach to selling U.S. citizens on military action in Iran that contrasts with his predecessors, seeking to avoid completely owning it.

item.title: Where Iran has retaliated across the Middle East, according to satellite images and videos
item.description: Iran intensified its retaliatory strikes against American allies in the Persian Gulf and Israel on Sunday, attacking sites in at least nine countries since the start of the joint U.S.-Israeli attack.

item.title: Man who killed 2 in Austin may have been motivated by attack on Iran, officials say
item.description: The gunman, who also wounded 14 others at a bar in Austin early Sunday before he was killed by officers at the scene, has been identified as a 53-year-old naturalized U.S. citizen who came to the country from Senegal in 2006, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: San Francisco Ballet cancels planned performances at the Kennedy Center
item.description: The ballet company joins a long list of groups and artists, including composer Philip Glass, soprano Renée Fleming and banjoist Béla Fleck, who have backed out of events at the cultural institution.
